title: Fix sensor reliability: GPIO4 migration, ADC saturation detection, baseline self-correction
description: MPXV7002DP pressure sensor on GPIO0 was unusable (floating ADC). Migrated to GPIO4, added ADC saturation trigger detection for high-baseline sensors, fixed baseline self-correction during idle, bidirectional trigger detection, and diagnostic voltage logging throughout countdown/trigger/calibration phases.
close_reason: Sensor working on GPIO4. ADC saturation detection, baseline self-correction during idle, bidirectional trigger, calibration rail check updated. Verified: calibration SUCCESS (delta=0.39V), countdown with no false starts, trigger at 0.400s reaction time.

title: Fix sensor reliability: ADC rail rejection reset, debounce revert, diagnostic logging
description: MPXV7002DP pressure sensor on GPIO0 unreliable. (1) ADC rail rejection (>3.2V) doesn't reset sensorAboveCount, letting corrupted debounce streaks carry forward. (2) DEBOUNCE_COUNT=5 too conservative for pressure tube. (3) No voltage logging during countdown/trigger for debugging.
close_reason: Implemented all 5 changes: (A) sensorTriggered() resets sensorAboveCount on ADC rail rejection, (B) DEBOUNCE_COUNT reverted to 3, (C) voltage/baseline/threshold logged each countdown second, (D) voltage logged on false start and real trigger, (E) calibration idle/press phases log every 10th sample. Build clean, 21/21 tests pass, flashed to device.

title: Remove browser auto calibration
description: Remove Auto Calibration from the device browser UI and remove browser/HTTP calibration endpoints while keeping diagnostic firmware calibration internals.
close_reason: Removed browser Auto Calibration UI, browser calibration HTTP endpoints, peer calibrate command UI/API, and updated tests/docs while preserving diagnostic firmware calibration internals.

title: Move network status to Network page
description: Move the Network Status panel from the Monitor/Results page to the top of the Network configuration page.
close_reason: Moved Network Status from Monitor/Results to the top of Network configuration and verified with tests/build.

title: Make peer clock sync transactional
description: Peer Commands should remove the separate Check Clock action. Clock Sync should perform an initial peer clock check, send/update peer clock sync, then confirm the peer reports an acceptable clock difference.
close_reason: Removed Check Clock and made Clock Sync perform initial ESP-NOW check, peer update, and tolerance-confirmed peer acknowledgement.

title: issue
description: When the gate trigger is triggered I want to hear a beep either high pitch for after the GO or deep before the GO

title: Make peer rider sync transactional
description: Peer Commands Sync Riders should send rider data to peer gates and then validate the peer state so the UI reports success only when the peer has the expected riders.
close_reason: Implemented transactional ESP-NOW rider sync validation with peer acknowledgements and verified with npm test plus firmware build.

title: Add LittleFS file browser to device UI
description: Add a Monitor > Files section that can browse LittleFS directories, traverse paths, and view file contents from the captive portal.
close_reason: Added Monitor > Files UI for LittleFS browsing and read-only viewing. Added GET /api/files and GET /api/files/view firmware endpoints with path normalization and capped file reads. Regenerated embedded UI. Verified npm test and make build.

title: Remove manual trigger delta control from normal UI
description: Auto Calibration now owns normal sensor calibration. Remove the manual Trigger Delta Above Baseline slider and save button from the Gate Config UI, but keep triggerDelta in config/API/backup restore because firmware and auto calibration depend on it. Update tests and embedded UI asset.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: removed manual Trigger Delta Above Baseline card, save button, and related JS handlers from normal Gate Config UI. Auto Calibration remains visible and triggerDelta stays in config/API/backup restore. Regenerated embedded UI. npm test and make build pass.

title: Delete active run on same-card NFC re-scan
description: When a rider taps the same NFC card during a stage started by that card, the gate should stop/cancel and delete the current active run immediately. Current firmware marks the active run Cancelled and clears activeRunId, but leaves the run in the queue until later terminal cleanup, so it can still appear as cancelled. Update same-card re-scan handling to remove the active run and verify.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: same-card re-scan now logs cancellation, clears active state, stops buzzer/baseline, and immediately removes the active run from RunQueue so it is stopped and deleted. Added static regression coverage. npm test and make build pass.

title: Allow same rider NFC re-scan to cancel active stage
description: Physical NFC scans are only read while activeRunId is empty, so the existing same-rider re-scan cancellation path is unreachable during countdown/on-course. Fix continuous NFC handling so a second swipe from the same rider cancels the active run/stage without accidental immediate cancel from a held tag, and verify with firmware build/tests.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: physical NFC scanning now runs during active stages and accepts only newly presented tags, so same-rider re-swipe reaches existing cancellation logic without held-tag immediate cancel. npm test and make build pass.

title: Implement firmware line-2 sensor timing path
description: Review follow-up from reviewThis.md: line-2 support is still only partially wired. Contracts, simulator, storage, UI config, and metrics reference line2/launchMs, but firmware/gate/src/main.cpp only defines SENSOR_LINE1_PIN and never samples a second start-gate sensor or calls RunQueue::stampLine2(). Define the line-2 hardware pin/ADC path, debounce/baseline/threshold behavior, when to stamp line2TriggeredAtMs during OnCourse, and add tests or hardware verification so launchMs is populated from the real device path instead of remaining null.

title: Minify and optimize device web interface performance
description: Reduce the size and load time of the embedded device web interface. Investigate minifying index.html, styles.css, main.js, and embedded docs/assets; removing unused CSS/JS; avoiding oversized on-device documentation payloads; using cache/no-cache headers intentionally; measuring firmware flash impact and first-load performance on Android/iPhone. Acceptance: device UI remains functional, embedded device_ui.h size is reduced or justified, make build passes, and tests cover generated asset embedding.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: embed script now minifies safe text formats, gzips embedded UI/docs assets, firmware serves them with Content-Encoding gzip, generated device_ui.h shrank from about 666 KB to 161 KB, flash use dropped to 47.7%, npm test/typecheck and make build pass.

title: Fix missing API documentation links in device web UI
description: Device web UI > Developer > API Documentation shows links for API Overview, OpenAPI (YAML), OpenAPI (JSON), curl Examples, Status Endpoint, and Riders Endpoint under the text 'Complete REST API reference for device control and integration.' These docs are missing or not served correctly from the firmware/web interface, so users cannot open the referenced documentation from the device UI. Verify which docs should be available on-device versus only in the repo, add firmware routes/embedded assets or adjust links accordingly, and ensure API docs include all current endpoints including config, peer ESP-NOW commands, events/runs/storage/sessions, NFC diagnostics, and calibration. Acceptance: every visible documentation link works from the device web UI, missing endpoint docs are added or links removed, docs/OpenAPI match current firmware routes, and tests cover link presence/serving behavior.
notes: Fixed on-device API docs: removed visible OpenAPI YAML link, added Config/Wi-Fi/Timing/Gate MAC endpoint docs alongside Status/Riders, embedded selected docs and OpenAPI JSON into device_ui.h, added firmware /docs/* routes, and added static + hardware endpoint test coverage. Verification: npm test, npm run typecheck, node --check apps/device-ui/main.js, make build. Build now uses 50.7% flash after embedded docs.
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Fix misleading sensor calibration threshold UI
description: Configuration > Gate Config > Sensor Calibration currently shows Start Sensor Threshold, Line 2 Sensor Threshold, and Finish Sensor Threshold as absolute 0.00-2.00 V values, with examples like 2, 2, and 1.05. This is misleading because current firmware triggering appears to use rolling baseline + triggerDelta/debounce rather than the stored per-sensor threshold fields as the active trigger decision path. Context: start/finish trigger behavior uses sensorTriggered(...) with baseline-relative delta; calibration updates triggerDelta. The UI still exposes startThreshold, line2Threshold, and finishThreshold, which may be stale from the old threshold-based model. Decide whether to restore per-sensor threshold semantics in firmware or update the UI/API/docs to expose the active calibration model. Acceptance: UI labels/help reflect actual firmware behavior, saved values affect triggering or are removed/hidden, docs/OpenAPI are consistent, and tests cover config display/save behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: UI now edits triggerDelta above rolling baseline, /api/config/time applies triggerDelta immediately, docs/OpenAPI and tests updated, npm test and make build pass.

title: Verify config backup restores gates after reset or hardware replacement
description: Validate that Device UI Download Config exports enough information to restore the Start Gate and all non-start gates after factory reset or replacement hardware. Cover Start, Finish, and Intermediate roles; gate number/derived role/device label, peer MAC/autodiscovery expectations, Wi-Fi/AP/station settings, channel, thresholds/calibration values, rider data if intended, event/session storage if intended, and secrets redaction limitations. Define expected restore flow from Config JSON, identify missing fields or fields that cannot be restored because passwords are redacted, and add tests/docs for full reset and new hardware scenarios.

title: Keep Developer Peer Tools page after peer command execution
description: Fix the Device UI behavior where executing a Developer > Peer Tools / Peer Commands action redirects away from the Peer Tools page. Expected behavior: command buttons call the local /api/peer/* endpoint, show the result in-place, refresh peer status without changing hash/page, and keep the user on #peer-tools. Likely area: apps/device-ui/main.js sendPeerCommand currently calls loadStatus(), whose role/page handling may navigate away from start-only pages or reset active state.
close_reason: Fixed: applyRoleUi() was redirecting away from start-only pages unconditionally on every loadStatus() call. Added !start guard so start gates stay on peer-tools page after executing commands.

title: Handle front-wheel lift during standing starts
description: Design and implement detection/mitigation for riders lifting the front wheel at launch. Current start timing depends on the pressure-tube start sensor trigger; a front-wheel lift can delay or skip the expected trigger and distort reaction/launch timing. Explore options such as dual start sensors, rear-wheel trigger semantics, false-start/window rules, UI/operator warnings, calibration guidance, and test coverage for wheel-lift scenarios.

title: Monitor serial output during API device tests
notes: Implemented serial monitoring for API device suites. Added shared attachSerialLogs helper and wired SerialLogWatcher into @api, @api-endpoints, @api-integration, @api-roles, and @api-validation after gate discovery so tests fail on serial error output. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test, git diff --check pass. Attempted npm run test:device:api; run failed before API assertions because configured /dev/ttyACM0 was not openable/no /dev/ttyACM* devices are visible in this environment.
close_reason: Implemented; hardware run blocked by missing serial device

title: Route peer tools over ESP-NOW commands
notes: Implemented ESP-NOW peer commands: added local Start Gate APIs /api/peer/ping, /api/peer/sync, /api/peer/calibrate, /api/peer/riders/sync; replaced browser peer HTTP console with Start-only Peer Commands UI; regenerated embedded device_ui.h; added static tests ensuring local ESP-NOW APIs are used instead of browser peer fetches. Verification: npm test, npm run typecheck, node --check apps/device-ui/main.js, make build.
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Modernize device web UI for mobile role-specific usability
notes: Implemented mobile-first static device UI: top status bar, phone bottom nav/desktop side nav, role-specific hiding for start-only rider surfaces, non-start managed-by-start notice, corrected Gate Start option, and regenerated embedded device_ui.h. Verification: npm test, npm run typecheck, node --check apps/device-ui/main.js, make build.
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Complete discussed web UI changes
notes:
Add in-page API Docs viewer to device UI so embedded docs are visible from the API Docs page instead of only linked.
Verified API Docs page fix with npm test and make build; firmware header regenerated successfully.

title: Destructive multi-gate hardware topology tests
notes: Fixed false-start timing: record actual pre-GO line-1 trigger timestamp and use it for Trigger-to-Finish/course timing while keeping the 5s penalty on Total. Firmware build passes.

title: LittleFS event storage - persistent JSONL event logs, session management, and API endpoints
notes: Implementation complete: partitions.csv, event_store.h/cpp, main.cpp modified. Build: 47.3% flash, 13% RAM. All 11 node tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Refactor duplication hotspots
close_reason: Implemented duplication refactor helpers, UI escaping, renderer tests; verified npm test, typecheck, JS syntax, make build

title: device-ui side-nav redesign
notes: Replaced tab SPA with side-nav multi-page layout. Pages: Results (home), Riders, Network, Gate Config, Reset, API Docs, Peer Tools. Hash-based routing. Peer Tools lets browser send GET/POST/PUT/DELETE to peer gate URLs. AGENTS.md updated.
close_reason: Closed

title: gate numbers 1-based; AP IP = 192.168.4.<gateNumber>
close_reason: Closed

title: AP SSID = deviceId always, remove apSsid as configurable field
close_reason: Closed

title: add gateNumber to device naming: Gate-<#>-<MAC>
notes: Added gateNumber (uint8_t, NVS key 'gateNum', default 0) to GateConfig. deviceId now built as Gate-<N>-<6hex> using last 3 bytes of efuse MAC. AP SSID defaults to deviceId (no MTBGate- prefix). Removed stale gate-unknown fallback. gateNumber exposed in configJson, settable via PUT /api/config/mac, updating deviceId immediately. UI: gate number input added to Peer Configuration section. Build: 66.2% flash, no errors.
close_reason: Closed

title: implement suggested improvements: fix docs, API error handling, UI queue wiring, XSS, typecheck
notes: all 5 items implemented: AGENTS.md corrected, JSON.parse try/catch, statusJson queue, renderAttempts DOM-safe, typecheck added. 9/9 tests pass, tsc clean, firmware 66.2% flash.
close_reason: Closed

title: Save review recommendations to suggestedImprovements.md
close_reason: Saved review recommendations to suggestedImprovements.md

acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md contains any unique guidance from AGENT.md and AGENT.md is removed.
notes: Merged unique AGENT.md product intent/use-case details into AGENTS.md and removed AGENT.md. Verified AGENT.md no longer exists and AGENTS.md contains the merged section.
close_reason: Merged AGENT.md content into AGENTS.md and removed duplicate file
